raw_response:
I agree with the previous assessment. While PCR works with nucleic acids but does so by amplifying DNA rather than focusing specifically on RNA, Sanger’s technique also deals exclusively with DNA sequencing without involving RNA processing steps. In contrast, next-generation sequencing often starts with converting messenger RNA into cDNA before proceeding to determine sequences, thus engaging more deeply with RNA processes compared to the others provided. Therefore:

Answer: C Next Generation Sequencing.
